commands.author: ericpearson

target.title: [Bug]: Discord channel sessions lose recent conversation context after reset, breaking organic continuity
target.body:
## Summary

Discord channel sessions can restart with no recent channel conversation context injected, even though the channel has a stable session key. The result is that the agent cannot organically pick up where the channel left off after an idle/session/gateway reset, and vague follow-up requests are interpreted against only the newest event instead of the prior conversation.

This is a user-facing continuity break in the main Discord experience, not just a polish issue. Users reasonably expect a dedicated channel/session to preserve or recover enough recent conversation to answer follow-ups naturally.

Related closed stale issue: #15807 (Feature: Auto-inject recent channel history on Discord session reset). This new issue has a fresh live repro from current behavior and should be treated as a P1 bug.

## Fresh live reproduction

Environment observed from a Discord guild channel on June 18, 2026 / June 19, 2026 UTC.

1. A Discord channel has an ongoing substantive thread.
2. Around 25 hours later, the user sends a new short message in the same channel.
3. The user then sends an ambiguous follow-up like `make a pic`, expecting the assistant to continue the prior substantive thread/context.
4. The assistant receives only the newest minimal Discord context and anchors on the immediately preceding message, forgetting the previous substantive thread.
5. Investigation showed:
   - Stable channel session key: `agent:main:discord:channel:<channel-id>`
   - Fresh live session created at the time of the newer message (`2026-06-19 01:42:15 UTC` in the observed case)
   - The prompt for the ambiguous follow-up contained only the current Discord metadata plus `make a pic`
   - It did not include terms/details from the previous thread
   - No explicit `session.reset` override was configured
   - `messages.groupChat.historyLimit` existed, but did not cause recent Discord channel history to be injected into the fresh session
   - Long-term memory was not injected for the guild channel (`injectedChars: 0`), which is consistent with Discord guild-channel privacy defaults

## Expected behavior

When a Discord guild/channel session is newly created or restored after idle/manual/gateway reset, OpenClaw should provide continuity for that same channel by default or via a clear binding-level setting. At minimum, the first turn after reset should have enough recent channel history to resolve short follow-ups like:

- `make a pic`
- `continue this`
- `what were we doing?`
- `can you fix that?`

The user should not have to manually tell the agent to read history every time a channel session resets.

## Actual behavior

A same-channel fresh session can start effectively blank, with only the newest inbound event and short assembled context. The agent behaves as if the previous conversation never happened. This makes channel sessions feel unreliable and causes embarrassing wrong-context responses.

## Why P1

This breaks the core promise of channel-based agents: a channel feels like a continuing conversation, but after reset it silently loses conversational continuity. The failure is highly visible, confusing, and easy for users to interpret as the assistant being careless or broken. It also creates repeated manual recovery work and support/debugging churn.

## Suggested fix shape

Add Discord binding/session recovery behavior that injects a bounded recent history window when a fresh session is created for a channel, with privacy-aware defaults. Possible shape:

```json
{
  "sessionRecovery": {
    "injectRecentChannelHistory": true,
    "messageCount": 15
  }
}
```

Important details:

- Scope to the same provider/account/channel binding.
- Preserve sender attribution in group chats.
- Avoid loading long-term private memory into guild channels unless already configured.
- Consider doing this only on fresh session creation/reset, not every turn.
- Make behavior visible in debug/session status so users can tell whether history was injected.

## Acceptance criteria

- A Discord channel session reset/restart followed by an ambiguous short follow-up includes recent same-channel context in the model input or an equivalent recovered context block.
- The history window is bounded and configurable.
- Guild-channel privacy boundaries are maintained.
- The feature works after idle resets, manual resets, and gateway restarts.
- There is a regression test covering a same-channel fresh session resolving a follow-up from recent channel history.
